memory_policy.purpose: convert thick derived evidence into executable code-generation memory before the next four-arm rerun

memory_expansions.composition_contract: Use one dominant promise field, one generated platform motif, and one controlled proof cue; reject equal feature panels.
memory_expansions.typography_contract: Headline must fit as one primary sentence with a secondary source-boundary caption hidden or minimized outside the public read.
memory_expansions.spacing_contract: Protect a large quiet field around the promise and keep product-family cues in a single controlled edge zone.
memory_expansions.motion_or_sequence_contract: Optional attention reset only: reveal promise first, then platform motif, then small proof cue.
memory_expansions.proof_object_contract: Generated native platform motif or product-route object; no source brand mark or copied event asset.
memory_expansions.code_generation_binding: native PPT code must call a launch identity field module before drawing cover/setup slides.
memory_expansions.negative_control_failure: bad control fails when the same sources produce equal boxes, feature catalogs, or visible workflow notes.
memory_expansions.release_boundary: public_blocked until source-brand sanitization and human cover review pass

memory_expansions.composition_contract: Largest visual zone is an original native product surface; method, trace, and caveat labels stay on a narrow secondary rail.
memory_expansions.typography_contract: Product-state labels use short verbs and nouns; no paragraphs inside the surface.
memory_expansions.spacing_contract: Surface must have a protected inspection area, a separate annotation gutter, and enough edge padding to avoid dashboard density.
memory_expansions.motion_or_sequence_contract: Use a bounded before-state to resolved-state sequence with reduced-motion fallback.
memory_expansions.proof_object_contract: Editable native product surface with state markers, not a raster screenshot or generic rectangle stack.
memory_expansions.code_generation_binding: native PPT code must bind selected surface state, annotation rail, and proof object geometry before rendering.
memory_expansions.negative_control_failure: bad control fails if it replaces product theater with checklists, UI copies, or flat card grids.
memory_expansions.release_boundary: public_blocked until editable-object QA and product-surface originality review pass

memory_expansions.composition_contract: Sequence is command, route, progress evidence, resolved state; only one path may be primary.
memory_expansions.typography_contract: Each state label is short and action-led; detailed explanation routes to notes or trace.
memory_expansions.spacing_contract: Keep path spacing consistent and reserve the resolved state as the largest endpoint.
memory_expansions.motion_or_sequence_contract: Ordered reveal metadata is required for each state even when the generated PPT remains static.
memory_expansions.proof_object_contract: Native state objects and path markers; no keynote frame, no copied product UI, no feature list.
memory_expansions.code_generation_binding: native PPT code must generate sequence states from explicit state data and record reveal order in trace.
memory_expansions.negative_control_failure: bad control fails if it names capabilities without command-route-result order.
memory_expansions.release_boundary: public_blocked until sequence order, source claim mapping, and human review pass

memory_expansions.composition_contract: One outcome object owns the climax; support evidence sits below or in trace, never as competing primary metrics.
memory_expansions.typography_contract: Metric or outcome wording must be source-backed, large enough for thumbnail read, and limited to one first-read object.
memory_expansions.spacing_contract: Use a clear stage field with generous margin and no dense table adjacent to the primary object.
memory_expansions.motion_or_sequence_contract: Optional scale emphasis on the outcome object; reduced-motion state must preserve the same hierarchy.
memory_expansions.proof_object_contract: Editable native metric or outcome object with caveat rail; no invented figures.
memory_expansions.code_generation_binding: native PPT code must require a verified outcome label and block rendering when metric provenance is absent.
memory_expansions.negative_control_failure: bad control fails if it creates multiple equal metrics, small tables, or unverified scale claims.
memory_expansions.release_boundary: public_blocked until exact claim verification and human approval pass

memory_expansions.composition_contract: Depth may support product evidence but cannot become decoration; foreground content remains the main read.
memory_expansions.typography_contract: All text over material surfaces must meet contrast and size checks before aesthetic effects are accepted.
memory_expansions.spacing_contract: Layered surfaces need protected content zones, highlight margins, and no overlap with primary text.
memory_expansions.motion_or_sequence_contract: Motion is bounded to attention reset, state reveal, or climax emphasis; reduced-motion fallback is mandatory.
memory_expansions.proof_object_contract: Native layered surface or generated SVG-like object that supports a product state.
memory_expansions.code_generation_binding: native PPT code must record material layer count, contrast probe, and fallback when depth is simplified.
memory_expansions.negative_control_failure: bad control fails when glass-like decoration appears without product evidence, contrast proof, or fallback.
memory_expansions.release_boundary: public_blocked until native render inspection and motion fallback review pass

memory_expansions.composition_contract: Every slide declares one claim, one proof object, and one reading route before visual styling.
memory_expansions.typography_contract: Type hierarchy is selected before palette: first-read claim, proof label, caveat, trace label.
memory_expansions.spacing_contract: Generator must choose macro zone, margin, gutter, and max visible word budget before drawing.
memory_expansions.motion_or_sequence_contract: Motion cannot compensate for weak reading order; sequence metadata must reinforce the declared route.
memory_expansions.proof_object_contract: Proof object has a protected area and cannot be replaced by decorative panels.
memory_expansions.code_generation_binding: native PPT code must receive spacing tokens and density budget as required inputs for every slide.
memory_expansions.negative_control_failure: bad control fails if it only changes colors, fonts, or box counts without enforcing hierarchy and spacing.
memory_expansions.release_boundary: public_blocked until layout geometry QA and human thumbnail review pass
